dashboard.subtitle: Here's what's happening in your workspace today.

hub.title: Help center
hub.subtitle: Answers, guides, and support for your Innexar Field workspace.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.description: Quick answers about billing, integrations, CRM, mobile, and the customer portal.

manual.title: System manual
manual.description: Step-by-step guides for daily workflows across your workspace.

support.title: Support
support.description: Contact our team or submit a ticket and track responses.

quickStart.title: New to Innexar Field?
quickStart.body: Start with workspace setup, modules, and your first customer record.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Browse by topic or open Support to reach our team.

subscription.q: How do I manage my SaaS subscription?
subscription.a: Open Settings → Billing or the Billing page from your dashboard. You can update your payment method, view invoices, and change plans through Stripe Checkout.

paymentMethods.q: Which payment methods are accepted for my workspace plan?
paymentMethods.a: Innexar Field uses Stripe for SaaS billing. Major credit and debit cards are supported; Apple Pay appears in Checkout when enabled in your Stripe Dashboard.

saasInvoices.q: Where do I find invoices for my Innexar Field subscription?
saasInvoices.a: Subscription invoices are available on the Billing page and emailed to your workspace owner. Customer invoices you send to clients are separate under Invoices.

connectStripe.q: How do I connect Stripe for customer payments?
connectStripe.a: Go to Settings → Integrations and click Connect Stripe. Complete Stripe Connect onboarding to accept card payments and receive payouts.

quickbooksSync.q: Can I sync with QuickBooks Online?
quickbooksSync.a: Yes. After your platform operator enables QuickBooks, connect from Settings → Integrations. Export invoices once your QuickBooks company is linked.

emailDelivery.a: Outbound email requires SMTP configured by your platform operator. Without SMTP, messages are logged only in development. See the integrations manual for details.

addCustomer.q: How do I add a new customer?
addCustomer.a: Open Customers → New customer. Enter contact details, billing preferences, and optional properties for service locations.

properties.q: What are properties on a customer record?
properties.a: Properties are service locations tied to a customer. They power estimates, job scheduling, and portal access per site.

pipeline.q: How does the sales pipeline work?
pipeline.a: Estimates move through draft, sent, accepted, or declined. Accepted estimates can convert to jobs without re-entering scope or pricing.

mobileAccess.q: How do field crews access the mobile app?
mobileAccess.a: Install the PWA from /m on a phone or tablet, or add it to the home screen. Crew members sign in with their workspace credentials.

offlineSync.q: Does the mobile app work offline?
offlineSync.a: Jobs and time entries cache locally. Sync when back online from the Sync page; conflicts follow last-write with server validation.

timeTracking.q: How do techs clock in on a job?
timeTracking.a: From Mobile → Time, select today's job and tap Clock in. Clock out when work is complete; hours feed payroll and job costing.

customerLogin.q: How do customers sign in to the portal?
customerLogin.a: Customers use magic-link email login at your portal URL with their company code. No password is required for standard portal access.

portalInvoices.q: What can customers see in the portal?
portalInvoices.a: Depending on your setup, customers can view invoices, payments, documents, bookings, and submit support requests.

portalSupport.q: How do portal support requests work?
portalSupport.a: Customers submit requests from Portal → Support. Your team receives them as threads under Portal → Messages and can respond by email.

manual.title: System manual
manual.subtitle: Structured guides for core Innexar Field workflows.

getting-started.title: Getting started
getting-started.summary: Set up your workspace and activate the modules you need.
getting-started.intro: Complete onboarding, choose industry packs, and invite your team before day-to-day operations.
getting-started.footer: Need help choosing modules? Visit Settings → Modules or contact support.

steps.step1: Finish onboarding: company profile, industry packs, and module selection.
steps.step2: Subscribe on the Billing step to activate your workspace.
steps.step3: Configure Settings → Integrations for payments and accounting when ready.
steps.step4: Add your first customer, then create an estimate or job.

customers.title: Customers
customers.summary: Manage client records, properties, and contact history.
customers.intro: Customers are the center of CRM, estimates, jobs, and invoicing in Innexar Field.
customers.footer: Customer portal access is configured per customer from their record.

steps.step1: Create a customer with legal name, email, and billing terms.
steps.step2: Add properties for each service location with address and access notes.
steps.step3: Link estimates and jobs to the correct property for accurate dispatch.
steps.step4: Enable portal access when the customer should self-serve invoices and bookings.

estimates.title: Estimates
estimates.summary: Build quotes, send proposals, and convert wins to jobs.
estimates.intro: Estimates pull from your price book and support room-based or flat pricing models.
estimates.footer: Accepted estimates can generate jobs without duplicate data entry.

steps.step1: Create an estimate from Customers or Estimates → New.
steps.step2: Add line items from the price book or custom lines.
steps.step3: Send the proposal link for e-signature or email delivery.
steps.step4: Track status: draft, sent, accepted, or declined.
steps.step5: Convert accepted estimates to jobs with one click.

dispatch.title: Dispatch & scheduling
dispatch.summary: Schedule crews, assign jobs, and monitor the board.
dispatch.intro: Dispatch connects jobs, crews, and calendars for field operations.
dispatch.footer: Mobile crews see assigned jobs under /m after dispatch.

steps.step1: Open the dispatcher board or Jobs calendar view.
steps.step2: Assign crews and time windows to scheduled jobs.
steps.step3: Notify techs via mobile; they confirm from the field app.
steps.step4: Reschedule or reassign from the board when plans change.

payroll.title: Payroll
payroll.summary: Review time entries and run payroll periods.
payroll.intro: Payroll aggregates approved time from jobs and mobile clock-ins.
payroll.footer: Export payroll data to your accounting integration when configured.

steps.step1: Ensure techs clock in and out on jobs from Mobile → Time.
steps.step2: Review and approve time entries for the pay period.
steps.step3: Run payroll preview to validate hours and rates.
steps.step4: Export or sync to accounting per your workspace setup.

portal.title: Customer portal
portal.summary: Give customers self-service access to invoices and support.
portal.intro: The portal uses magic-link login and respects tenant branding.
portal.footer: Portal URLs use your workspace company code for customer sign-in.

steps.step1: Enable portal access on eligible customer records.
steps.step2: Share the portal URL and company code with customers.
steps.step3: Customers sign in via email magic link — no password required.
steps.step4: Monitor portal support requests under Portal → Messages.

admin.title: Workspace admin
admin.summary: Users, modules, billing, and workspace settings.
admin.intro: Owners and admins manage the tenant from Settings and Billing.
admin.footer: Platform-wide credentials are managed by your Innexar operator in Admin.

steps.step1: Manage modules under Settings → Modules after onboarding.
steps.step2: Invite users and assign roles from Settings → Users.
steps.step3: Configure SaaS billing and payment method on the Billing page.
steps.step4: Connect integrations when your operator has saved platform credentials.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.summary: Stripe Connect, QuickBooks, Avalara, and email delivery.
integrations.intro: Platform operators configure API credentials in Admin; workspace admins connect tenant accounts here.
integrations.footer: Need platform credential changes? Contact your Innexar Field operator.

steps.step1: Open Settings → Integrations to view connection status.
steps.step2: Connect Stripe or QuickBooks with OAuth flows on each card.
steps.step3: Verify tax preview and test email templates when live credentials exist.
steps.step4: Use this manual for detailed steps per integration below.

quickbooks.title: QuickBooks Online
quickbooks.summary: Sync customers, invoices, and payments with QuickBooks.

steps.step1: Platform: enable QuickBooks and store Intuit OAuth credentials in Admin.
steps.step2: Tenant: click Connect QuickBooks and sign in with Intuit.
steps.step3: Select the QuickBooks company to link; OAuth returns automatically.
steps.step4: Export invoices once the realm ID shows as connected.
steps.step5: Disconnect before linking a different QuickBooks company.

avalara.title: Avalara AvaTax
avalara.summary: Automated US sales tax on quotes and invoices.

steps.step1: Platform: save Avalara Account ID and License key in Admin.
steps.step2: Development may use mock tax rates when live credentials are absent.
steps.step3: Tenant: use Preview tax on the Integrations card to validate rates.

smtp.title: Email (SMTP)
smtp.summary: Outbound email for invoices, reminders, and magic links.

steps.step1: Platform: enable SMTP in Admin with host, port, and credentials.
steps.step2: Without SMTP, email is log-only — useful for local development.
steps.step3: Tenant: send a test from Settings → Email templates after SMTP is live.

integrations.helpIntro: Configure provider credentials here before tenants connect Stripe Connect or QuickBooks from Settings → Integrations. Secrets are encrypted at rest; leave a field blank to keep the existing value.

stripe.help: Required for SaaS billing and Stripe Connect onboarding. Use live or test keys from the Stripe Dashboard. Webhook secret verifies billing events.

quickbooks.title: QuickBooks
quickbooks.help: Create an Intuit Developer app with OAuth 2.0 redirect URI pointing to your API callback. Tenants connect their own QuickBooks company after these platform credentials are saved.

avalara.title: Avalara
avalara.help: Optional live sales tax. When disabled or without credentials, tenants may see mock rates in development (debug.mock_avalara).

smtp.title: Email / SMTP
smtp.help: Optional — enables outbound email for magic links, invoices, and template test sends. When disabled, messages are logged only.
smtp.optionalHint: Optional — for magic links and transactional email.

storage.title: R2 / S3 storage
storage.help: Object storage for uploads, photos, and documents. Configure bucket and public URL for signed or CDN-backed assets.
